name: Cross-Site Scripting (Reflected XSS Detection)
description: Tests for reflected XSS by injecting script payloads into URL parameters and checking if they appear unescaped in the response body. Detects missing output encoding and Content Security Policy gaps.
author: AC3 ScanForge
remediation: Implement context-aware output encoding for all user-supplied data. Deploy Content Security Policy (CSP) headers. Use HTTPOnly and Secure flags on session cookies. Consider using a templating engine with auto-escaping.

intelligence.dfirRelevance: XSS is commonly used for session hijacking, credential theft via phishing overlays, and as a stepping stone to more complex attacks like CSRF or drive-by downloads.
